reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Column 0 vs column 31 — both edges are predominantly dark green with sparse flower pixels; the color values appear to match closely, no hard seam break. (2) Row 0 vs row 31 — top and bottom rows are similarly green-dominant with scattered small flower fragments; no obvious discontinuity. (3) 3x3 mental tile test — this is where the problem surfaces: the pink/salmon flower shapes form a loose diagonal band running from upper-left toward lower-right across the 32x32 area. When tiled, this diagonal band concatenates into a repeating diagonal stripe that draws the eye across the full tiled surface. Not a polka-dot but a macro stripe artifact. DENSITY: The flower mass is slightly concentrated in the upper-center-to-center region; lower-right quadrant is noticeably sparser/greener, creating subtle light-dark rhythm on repetition. BACKGROUND QUALITY: The dark green base is well-chosen and would recede behind sprites, but the large pink/salmon fragments (some spanning ~4-5px) are vivid enough to compete with small foreground elements. Edge seamlessness is good; the primary problem is the diagonal macro-pattern that violates the 'no dominant focal feature' criterion for a tiling texture.
